senses.definition: to check a web page, text, etc. without  reading all the content

examples.x: He browsed online forums to read reviews and opinions about the latest video games.
He browsed the internet for hours, looking for information on his favorite topic.
She likes to browse online stores for hours, searching for the perfect gifts.
He browsed various news websites to stay updated on current events.
We browsed the web for restaurant reviews before deciding where to dine out.

senses.definition: to break a telephone conversation between two people

examples.x: I accidentally disconnected the call when I pressed the wrong button on my phone.
He decided to disconnect the call after realizing he dialed the wrong number.
She chose to disconnect the call rather than engage in an argument with her colleague.
I had to disconnect the call because of a poor signal in the area I was in.

senses.definition: (computing) to move from one website to another or find one's way around on a website

examples.x: Students were taught how to navigate the educational platform to access course materials.
During the online workshop, participants were guided on how to navigate the webinar platform.
Users can navigate the social media platform to connect with friends, share updates, and explore trending topics.
The job seeker navigated the career portal, searching for relevant job postings.

senses.definition: to ruin someone's photo by making a sudden appearance or a silly face as the picture is about to be taken

examples.x: The group of friends decided to photobomb the tourist's picture at the landmark.

senses.definition: to send an unwanted advertisement or message online, usually to a lot of people

examples.x: The company's marketing team decided to spam their email subscribers with promotional offers.
She warned him not to spam the forum with irrelevant posts or advertisements.
The company's email server was overwhelmed after a spammer attempted to spam their inbox with thousands of unsolicited messages.

senses.definition: to remove restrictions so that a website, phone, account, etc. can be accessed again

examples.x: After resolving the security issue, the IT department decided to unblock access to the restricted website for employees.
She contacted customer support to request them to unblock her account after it was mistakenly flagged for suspicious activity.
The administrator had to unblock the firewall settings to allow the new software update to be downloaded and installed.
He asked the network administrator to unblock his smartphone so he could access certain apps that were previously restricted.
The school's internet filter accidentally blocked access to educational websites, prompting the IT department to quickly unblock them.

senses.definition: related to a telephone system that uses radio stations for communication

examples.x: The cellular network coverage in rural areas is not as extensive as in urban areas.
The company invested in upgrading its cellular infrastructure to provide faster and more reliable service to customers.
He forgot to charge his cellular device, leaving him without means of communication during the power outage.
The cellular technology allows for seamless handoffs between different base stations while traveling.

senses.definition: not functioning because of having no power

examples.x: The flashlight went dead during the hike.
His phone was dead before noon.
The engine was dead and wouldn't start.
All the streetlights were dead after the outage.
The computer screen was dead.

senses.definition: a system of Internet connection that allows users to share information simultaneously

examples.x: She relies on broadband for her work, as it allows her to upload and download large files quickly.
The rural community finally gained access to broadband, opening up new opportunities for remote work and online education.
Students in the classroom can access online resources simultaneously thanks to the school's broadband connection.
The broadband connection at the conference center ensures that attendees can livestream presentations without interruption.

senses.definition: a type of television or radio program in which the audience take part by calling the studio to voice their opinions

examples.x: The call-in show on the local radio station invites listeners to share their thoughts on current events and community issues.
She enjoys listening to the call-in program on politics, where people from all walks of life express their viewpoints.
The TV network launched a new call-in segment to engage viewers and encourage interactive participation.
The call-in segment of the talk show allows audience members to pose questions to the guest experts.
He dialed into the call-in radio program to share his personal experiences and offer advice to others in similar situations.

senses.definition: (computing) data that a web server sends to a browser and receives if the user visits the website again, used for identifying or tracking the user's activities

examples.x: Websites use cookies to remember users' preferences and provide personalized experiences during their visits.
He cleared his browser's cookies to ensure his online activities weren't being tracked by advertisers.
The website prompted users to accept cookies as part of its privacy policy before accessing the content.
She disabled third-party cookies in her browser settings to enhance her online privacy.
The website's use of cookies allows it to analyze user behavior and improve its services over time.

senses.definition: the last characters of a website's address such as '.com', '.org', etc.

examples.x: The website's domain is crucial for establishing its online identity and presence.
He registered a new domain for his business website, choosing '.net' to reflect its networking services.
The organization opted for a '.org' domain to signify its nonprofit status and commitment to social causes.
Users can search for available domains using domain registration services to find the perfect match for their website.
The domain name registrar offers various options for domain extensions, including country-specific ones like '.uk' or '.ca'.

senses.definition: a small digital icon or image used on social media or in text messages to show the tone of a message or its sender's facial expression, originated in Japan

examples.x: He used a thumbs-up emoji to indicate his approval of the plan.
She sent a series of laughing emoji in response to the funny meme shared by her friend.

senses.definition: a sign formed by keyboard characters to show the tone of a message or its sender's facial expression, used on social media or in text messages

examples.x: She added a smiley emoticon at the end of her message to indicate friendliness.
The email included a winking emoticon to convey humor.
He used a sad emoticon to express sympathy for his friend's situation.
The chat conversation was filled with various emoticons representing laughter, surprise, and confusion.
The use of emoticons in text messaging has become a popular way to enhance communication and convey tone.

senses.definition: used especially in text or online messages to express laughter or amusement about something funny
used to express shock, surprise, or excitement, particularly on social media or in text messages
a telephone in a public place that one needs to pay for, mostly by prepaid cards

examples.x: He searched for a payphone to call for help after his car broke down on the highway.
The hotel lobby still had a functional payphone for guests who didn't have cell phones.
She dug through her purse for change to use the payphone to inform her parents of her late arrival.
The payphone booth on the corner was a relic of the past, rarely used in the age of smartphones.
He used the payphone outside the convenience store to call his friend and arrange a meeting spot.

senses.definition: a communication system by which people were able to send and receive messages over long distances, using electrical or radio signals

examples.x: The old train station still has a room dedicated to the telegraph, showcasing relics from the past.
He visited the museum to learn about the history of the telegraph and its impact on communication.

senses.definition: (computing) an area on a computer containing files that are necessary for keeping the computer organized

examples.x: The IT technician organized the files into separate directories to improve file management.
She created a new directory on her computer desktop to store all her photos from the vacation.
He accidentally deleted an important directory, causing him to lose access to critical documents.
She used the search function to quickly locate a specific file within the directory.
The directory tree displayed the hierarchical structure of folders and subfolders on the computer.

senses.definition: a group of numbered buttons on a surface used for operating a TV, phone, computer, etc.

examples.x: The security system required a passcode to be entered on the keypad before granting access.
She dialed the phone number using the keypad on her mobile device.
The ATM machine had a touch-sensitive keypad for entering PIN numbers.
He pressed the buttons on the keypad to unlock the door to the office.
The remote control for the television had a numeric keypad for channel selection.

senses.definition: an extra telephone connected to the main phone line in a house

examples.x: She picked up the extension in the bedroom to answer the call while her husband spoke on the main line.
He dialed the extension to reach his colleague in the neighboring cubicle.
They installed a new phone system with multiple extensions to accommodate the growing workforce.
The technician tested the phone lines to ensure each extension was properly connected.
The main line was down, so he used his mobile phone to call the office extension instead.

senses.definition: a device that can send and receive documents in electronic form using a telephone line and then print them

examples.x: He sent the contract via fax to ensure it reached the client promptly.
The fax machine beeped as it received the document from the sender.
The office has a dedicated fax line for sending and receiving important documents.
The fax machine at the doctor's office hummed quietly as it transmitted medical records.

senses.definition: a communication system that allows people in different parts of a plane, office, etc. to speak to each other

examples.x: The pilot used the intercom to update passengers on the flight status and weather conditions.
The intercom in the office allowed employees to communicate with each other without leaving their desks.
She buzzed the receptionist using the intercom to announce her arrival at the building entrance.
The security guard used the intercom to verify the identity of visitors before granting them access to the building.

senses.definition: a meeting held among several people who are in different locations, linked via the Internet

examples.x: The teleconference allowed team members from different locations to collaborate on the project.
She joined the teleconference from her home office using the video conferencing software.
The quarterly meeting was conducted via teleconference to accommodate remote attendees.
Participants logged into the teleconference using a secure link provided by the organizer.
The teleconference was scheduled to discuss the company's financial performance and future goals.

senses.definition: the connection and exchange of data between everyday objects over the Internet
a video, image, etc. that has been altered to act as a joke or to tease someone, used and quickly spread by internet users

examples.x: She created a hilarious meme featuring her pet that quickly became a internet sensation.
The meme went viral on social media, garnering thousands of shares and likes.
Memes are often used to convey humor, satire, or cultural references in a concise and shareable format.
The meme featured a popular catchphrase from a television show, accompanied by a relevant image.
He shared a meme with his friends that perfectly captured their inside joke.

senses.definition: a phone that is a cross between a mobile phone and a tablet, larger than the former but smaller than the latter

examples.x: The phablet combines the features of a smartphone and a tablet, offering users a larger screen for multimedia consumption.
She opted for a phablet instead of a traditional smartphone for its larger display and enhanced productivity capabilities.
Phablets have gained popularity among consumers who prioritize screen size for gaming, streaming, and reading.
The phablet's dual functionality makes it a versatile device for both work and entertainment.
He struggled to find a comfortable grip on the phablet due to its larger size compared to his previous smartphone.

senses.definition: a photo of a person that is taken by the same person, usually shared on social media

examples.x: She snapped a quick selfie with her friends at the beach to capture the moment.
Selfies have become a ubiquitous form of self-expression on social media platforms.
The selfie received hundreds of likes and comments from followers on her Instagram account.
Taking a selfie with the scenic backdrop of the mountains was a highlight of their hiking trip.
She practiced her best smile before taking a selfie to share with her family.

senses.definition: a form of video content, often published on websites or social media platforms, where individuals or content creators share their personal experiences, expertise, or opinions through recorded videos in a blog-like format

examples.x: She started a vlog to document her travels and share her experiences with viewers.
Vlogs offer a more personal and interactive way for content creators to engage with their audience.
He uploads a new vlog every week, sharing behind-the-scenes footage of his creative process.
The vlog provides insights into his daily life, hobbies, and adventures around the city.
The vlog format allows for spontaneous and unscripted storytelling, fostering a sense of authenticity.

senses.definition: a sensational or misleading online content designed to attract clicks
a website that is regularly updated by its owner or owners and gives information about a specific topic or things that happen to them

examples.x: She started her own weblog to share her thoughts on fashion, beauty, and lifestyle trends.
He updates his weblog regularly with articles, essays, and personal reflections on current events.
The weblog features guest posts from industry experts, providing diverse perspectives on relevant topics.
She monetizes her weblog through sponsored content, affiliate marketing, and ad revenue.

senses.definition: the use of electronic communication to harass, intimidate, or harm others, typically through social media, messaging apps, or online platforms

examples.x: Cyberbullying involves using digital platforms such as social media, texts, or emails to harass, intimidate, or harm others.
Cyberbullying can take various forms, including spreading rumors, sharing embarrassing photos or videos, or sending threatening messages online.
